current_run.description: Given an N-bit input x, an oracle f_s(x) computes the logical OR of a contiguous cyclic segment of bits from x. The secret s encodes two parameters: s_idx, the starting index of the segment (0 to N-1), and k_val, the length of the segment (2 to N). The oracle returns 1 if at least one bit in the segment x_s_idx, x_(s_idx+1)%N, ..., x_(s_idx+k_val-1)%N is 1, and 0 otherwise. The goal is to determine s_idx and k_val. This function is equivalent to a boolean polynomial of degree k_val over GF(2). Note: If k_val equals N, the function is the OR of all bits, and s_idx is effectively irrelevant; in this case, s_idx is canonically set to 0 for unique secret representation.
